([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Archidioecesis Mechliniensis–Bruxellensis*) is a [Latin Church](/wiki/Latin_Church "Latin Church") ecclesiastical territory or [archdiocese](/wiki/Archdiocese "Archdiocese") of the [Catholic Church in Belgium](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium"). It is the [primatial](/wiki/Primate_(bishop) "Primate (bishop)") [see](/wiki/Episcopal_See "Episcopal See") of the whole of Belgium. Additionally it is the centre of the local [ecclesiastical province](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") governed by the Archbishop of Mechelen–Brussels, which covers the middle of the country corresponding to the now defunct Belgian [Province of Brabant](/wiki/Province_of_Brabant "Province of Brabant") and a few other municipalities adjacent to it.

The **Archdiocese of Mechelen–Brussels** ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Archidioecesis Mechliniensis–Bruxellensis*) is a [Latin Church](/wiki/Latin_Church "Latin Church") ecclesiastical territory or [archdiocese](/wiki/Archdiocese "Archdiocese") of the [Catholic Church in Belgium](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium"). It is the [primatial](/wiki/Primate_(bishop) "Primate (bishop)") [see](/wiki/Episcopal_See "Episcopal See") of the whole of Belgium. Additionally it is the centre of the local [ecclesiastical province](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") governed by the Archbishop of Mechelen–Brussels, which covers the middle of the country corresponding to the now defunct Belgian [Province of Brabant](/wiki/Province_of_Brabant "Province of Brabant") and a few other municipalities adjacent to it.

The Archdiocese was formed in 1559, and the [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") has a [seat](/wiki/Cathedra "Cathedra") in two cathedrals, [St. Rumbold's Cathedral](/wiki/St._Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St. Rumbold's Cathedral") in [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") and the [Cathedral of St. Michael and St. Gudula](/wiki/Cathedral_of_St._Michael_and_St._Gudula "Cathedral of St. Michael and St. Gudula") in [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"). The current archbishop is [Luc Terlinden](/wiki/Luc_Terlinden "Luc Terlinden"), who was installed in September 2023.

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/Saints-Michel-et-Gudule_Luc_Viatour.jpg/330px-Saints-Michel-et-Gudule_Luc_Viatour.jpg)](/wiki/File:Saints-Michel-et-Gudule_Luc_Viatour.jpg)

[St. Michael and St. Gudula Cathedral](/wiki/St._Michael_and_St._Gudula_Cathedral "St. Michael and St. Gudula Cathedral"), [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels")

The Archdiocese of Mechelen–Brussels consists of an area corresponding to the former Province of Brabant in addition to eight [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") in the [Province of Antwerp](/wiki/Province_of_Antwerp "Province of Antwerp"), including [Bonheiden](/wiki/Bonheiden "Bonheiden"), [Duffel](/wiki/Duffel "Duffel"), [Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") and [Sint-Katelijne-Waver](/wiki/Sint-Katelijne-Waver "Sint-Katelijne-Waver").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

In 1995, then existing Province of Brabant was split into three areas in a Belgian government reorganization:

* the Dutch-speaking province of [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant");
* the bilingual [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels-Capital_Region "Brussels-Capital Region");
* the French-speaking province of [Walloon Brabant](/wiki/Walloon_Brabant "Walloon Brabant").

The Church did not form new dioceses to fit with this; instead, three vicariates general were created, with their own [auxiliary bishop](/wiki/Auxiliary_bishop "Auxiliary bishop"), to accommodate the three regional entities.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Language issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Language issues")]

The name differs in the diocese's two languages; the Dutch name of the see is *Mechelen–Brussel* and in French, it is called *Malines–Bruxelles*.

[Mechelen](/wiki/Mechelen "Mechelen") was traditionally called *Mechlin* or *Malines* in English, but in recent decades the modern Dutch variant has become the norm.

## Cathedrals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cathedrals")]

Main article: [Co-cathedral](/wiki/Co-cathedral "Co-cathedral")

The duality of the Belgian archbishopric is also reflected in its status with two active [co-cathedrals](/wiki/Co-cathedral "Co-cathedral"): with [St. Rumbold's](/wiki/St._Rumbold%27s_Cathedral "St. Rumbold's Cathedral") in Mechelen and [St. Michael and St. Gudula](/wiki/St._Michael_and_St._Gudula_Cathedral "St. Michael and St. Gudula Cathedral") in Brussels.

## Collegiate churches and chapters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Collegiate churches and chapters")]

Former chapters in the archdiocese.

* Chapter of Our-Lady: Cathedral of Mechelen
* Chapter of Saint-Peter: Leuven
* Chapter of Saint John the Baptist: Diest
* Chapter of Saint-Sulpicius: Diest
* Chapter of Our-Lady: Aarschot
* Chapter of Saint-Leonard: Zouleeuw
* Chapter of Saint-Germanus: Tienen

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: History")]

The Archbishop of Mechelen was historically [primate](/wiki/Primate_(bishop) "Primate (bishop)") of the whole of the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries") following the 1559 reorganisation creating fifteen dioceses. Over time, the two other ecclesiastical provinces broke from Mechelen's primacy. [Cambrai](/wiki/Cambrai "Cambrai") was already in France and its kings managed gradually to annex southern French Flanders, and [Utrecht](/wiki/Utrecht_(province) "Utrecht (province)") and its suffragans in the Dutch republic (later kingdom) would long have their hierarchy suspended because the northern state broke away to be the champion of "anti-papist" Calvinism. The Napoleonic 1801 concordat re-drew the whole map again.

The country, by tradition, has the Archbishop of Mechelen made a cardinal.

The Archdiocese of Mechelen was renamed the Archdiocese of Mechelen–Brussels on 8 December 1961 as part of a restructuring of the Catholic dioceses in Belgium.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Two new dioceses were created. On the same day, the [Diocese of Antwerp](/wiki/Diocese_of_Antwerp "Diocese of Antwerp") was created from areas previously administered by the Archdiocese of Mechelen. Six years later the [Diocese of Hasselt](/wiki/Diocese_of_Hasselt "Diocese of Hasselt") was also created.[[4]](#cite_note-4) This meant that the new dioceses largely corresponding to the [provinces of Belgium](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium"). Most of the Catholic Church's presence in the [Province of Antwerp](/wiki/Province_of_Antwerp "Province of Antwerp") (except in the municipality of Mechelen) was made into the [Diocese of Antwerp](/wiki/Diocese_of_Antwerp "Diocese of Antwerp").

Archbishop André-Joseph Leonard succeeded Cardinal Danneels in January 2010. On 22 February 2011, [Pope Benedict XVI](/wiki/Pope_Benedict_XVI "Pope Benedict XVI") appointed Fr. [Jean Kockerols](/wiki/Jean_Kockerols "Jean Kockerols"), Fr. [Jean-Luc Hudsyn](/wiki/Jean-Luc_Hudsyn "Jean-Luc Hudsyn"), and Fr. [Leon Lemmens](/wiki/Leon_Lemmens "Leon Lemmens") as [auxiliary bishops](/wiki/Auxiliary_bishop "Auxiliary bishop") of the Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Upon reaching 75 years Leonard tendered his resignation, which was accepted. In the autumn of 2015 Pope Francis appointed the bishop of Bruges, Jozef De Kesel, as the new archbishop, who was created Cardinal in 2016. De Kesel was succeeded on 3 September 2023 by [Luc Terlinden](/wiki/Luc_Terlinden "Luc Terlinden").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Heraldry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Heraldry")]

Coat of arms of Archdiocese of Mechelen–Brussels

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Adopted  1961, when the name of the archdiocese was changed.[[7]](#cite_note-heraldrywiki-7)[[8]](#cite_note-heraldrybook-8)  Escutcheon  Quarterly 1st and 4th, Or a lion rampant Gules armed and langued Azure within a double tressure flory-counter-flory of the second ([Scotland](/wiki/Royal_Arms_of_Scotland "Royal Arms of Scotland")), 2nd and 3rd quarterly, Gules St-Michael Or, slaying the demon Sable ([the City of Brussels](/wiki/The_City_of_Brussels "The City of Brussels")).[[7]](#cite_note-heraldrywiki-7)[[8]](#cite_note-heraldrybook-8)[[9]](#cite_note-COA-9)  Previous versions  From 1559 to 1961: Or a lion rampant Gules armed and langued Azure within a double tressure flory-counter-flory of the second ([Scotland](/wiki/Royal_Arms_of_Scotland "Royal Arms of Scotland"))[[7]](#cite_note-heraldrywiki-7)[[8]](#cite_note-heraldrybook-8) |

* [Victor-Auguste-Isidore Dechamps](/wiki/Victor-Auguste-Isidore_Dechamps "Victor-Auguste-Isidore Dechamps"), C.SS.R. (priest here, 1834–1836), appointed Bishop of Namur {Namen} in 1865; later returned here as Archbishop; future Cardinal
* [Maximilien de Fürstenberg](/wiki/Maximilien_de_F%C3%BCrstenberg "Maximilien de Fürstenberg"), appointed apostolic delegate and titular archbishop in 1949; future Cardinal
* [Louis-Joseph Delebecque](/wiki/Louis-Joseph_Delebecque "Louis-Joseph Delebecque"), appointed Bishop of Ghent in 1838
* [Pierre-Lambert Goossens](/wiki/Pierre-Lambert_Goossens "Pierre-Lambert Goossens"), appointed Coadjutor Bishop of Namur in 1883; later returned here as Archbishop; future Cardinal
* [Jean Jadot](/wiki/Jean_Jadot "Jean Jadot"), appointed apostolic delegate and titular archbishop in 1968
